title: Century: Spice Road
publisher: Plan B Games / Next Move Games

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Cards
- **36** Point Cards
- **53** Merchant Cards

#### Cubes & Containers
- **105** wooden Spice Cubes total
- **4 colors** of cubes: yellow turmeric, red saffron, green cardamom, brown cinnamon
- **4** plastic Spice Bowls

#### Coins & Player Aids
- **20** Silver Coins
- **10** Gold Coins
- **5** Caravan Cards

#### Quick Function Guide
- **Merchant Cards** — Build your production/trade/upgrade engine.
- **Point Cards** — Main way to score victory points.
- **Spice Cubes** — Core currency used to trade, upgrade, and claim points.
- **Coins** — Bonus points for claiming certain point cards earlier.
- **Caravan Cards** — Personal area showing your spice inventory space.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Shared Starting Hand (All Players)
- **Caravan card** — Take 1 and place it face up in front of you.
- **Card 1** — Take the basic Production card (gain 2 yellow cubes).
- **Card 2** — Take the basic Upgrade card (2 total upgrade steps).

#### Player-Count Audit Reference (from rules used in this file)
- **Starting cubes vary by seat order** — Values differ for seats 1-5.
- **End trigger varies by player count** — 2-3 players vs 4-5 players use different point-card thresholds.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Seat 1** — Start with 3 yellow cubes.
- **Seat 2** — Start with 4 yellow cubes.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Seat 1** — Start with 3 yellow cubes.
- **Seat 2** — Start with 4 yellow cubes.
- **Seat 3** — Start with 4 yellow cubes.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Seat 1** — Start with 3 yellow cubes.
- **Seat 2** — Start with 4 yellow cubes.
- **Seat 3** — Start with 4 yellow cubes.
- **Seat 4** — Start with 3 yellow + 1 red cube.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Seat 1** — Start with 3 yellow cubes.
- **Seat 2** — Start with 4 yellow cubes.
- **Seat 3** — Start with 4 yellow cubes.
- **Seat 4** — Start with 3 yellow + 1 red cube.
- **Seat 5** — Start with 3 yellow + 1 red cube.

#### Capacity Reminder
- **Inventory limit** — Keep at most 10 spice cubes unless a variant says otherwise.
- **Overflow rule** — If above capacity, discard down immediately.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Merchant row** — Shuffle Merchant deck and reveal 6 cards left-to-right.
2. **Point row** — Shuffle Point deck and reveal 5 cards left-to-right.
3. **Coin bonuses** — Put 1 Gold Coin on leftmost point card.
4. **Coin bonuses** — Put 1 Silver Coin on second point card.
5. **Coin supply** — Place remaining coins near the table as general supply.
6. **First player** — Choose randomly, then play clockwise.

#### Before Turn 1 Check
- **Starting hands** — Each player has only the 2 basic merchant cards.
- **Discard piles** — No player has cards in discard yet.
- **Rows open** — Merchant row and point row are fully visible to everyone.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose one action** — Play a Merchant Card from hand.
2. **Choose one action** — Acquire a Merchant Card from the row.
3. **Choose one action** — Claim a Point Card from the row.
4. **Choose one action** — Rest and recover played Merchant Cards.

#### Turn Limits
- **One action only** — You do exactly one of the four actions each turn.
- **No carryover actions** — Extra actions do not exist unless a card effect explicitly grants one.
- **Pass order** — After resolution, turn passes clockwise.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Action 1: Play a Merchant Card
- **Play source** — Choose exactly 1 card from your hand.
- **Placement** — Put it face up in your played area.
- **Resolve text** — Apply the printed effect once unless card text allows repeats.

#### Merchant Effect Types
- **Production cards** — Gain cubes exactly as shown.
- **Trade cards** — Pay input cubes to gain output cubes shown.
- **Trade repeats** — You may repeat the same trade in that play if you can pay each full input.
- **Upgrade cards** — Move cubes up the value ladder: yellow to red to green to brown.
- **Upgrade split** — You may divide upgrade steps across multiple cubes.

#### Capacity During Resolution
- **Overflow check** — If a card effect pushes you over capacity, discard excess immediately.

#### Action 2: Acquire a Merchant Card
- **Take one card** — Choose 1 of the 6 face-up merchant cards.
- **Leftmost card** — Costs 0 cubes.
- **Deeper card cost** — Place 1 cube on each skipped card to the left.
- **Payment requirement** — You must pay every skipped position to take that card.
- **Bonus cubes** — If the chosen card has cubes on it, take them immediately.
- **Card destination** — Put acquired merchant card into your hand.
- **Row refill** — Slide remaining cards left and refill rightmost slot from deck.

#### Action 3: Claim a Point Card
- **Choose one card** — Take 1 face-up point card.
- **Exact payment** — Pay the exact spice combination shown.
- **Scoring area** — Place claimed point card in front of you.
- **Coin bonus** — Take any coin on that card.
- **Row refill** — Refill point row back to 5 cards when possible.

#### Action 4: Rest
- **Recover cards** — Take all cards from played area back into hand.
- **No bonus gain** — Rest does not directly grant cubes or points.

#### Legality Checks
- **Hand restriction** — You can only play cards currently in your hand.
- **No partial claims** — You cannot partially pay for a point card.
- **One claim max** — You can claim only one point card per turn because turn = one action.
- **Action freedom** — You may repeat same action type on consecutive turns if legal.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Merchant Row Tax Pressure
- **Skip cost** — Buying deeper cards costs cubes placed onto earlier cards.
- **Opportunity leak** — Those cubes become potential value for opponents.
- **Tempo tradeoff** — You trade immediate premium-card access for short-term resource loss.

#### Rest Timing Engine
- **Card lockout** — Played cards stay unavailable until you Rest.
- **Cycle planning** — Strong turns come from sequencing plays before a high-value Rest.
- **Hand rhythm** — Efficient players minimize low-value emergency rests.

#### Spice Ladder Economy
- **Direction only** — Upgrades move one-way from yellow to brown.
- **Value concentration** — High-value point cards usually require more green/brown cubes.
- **Infrastructure need** — Upgrade effects are often mandatory for consistent scoring pace.

#### Coin Race
- **Early claim reward** — Left-side point cards grant coin bonuses.
- **Scoring cushion** — Fast claims can create an early VP lead before full engines appear.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger by Player Count
--- 2 Players ---
- **Trigger** — Endgame starts when any player has claimed 6 point cards.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Trigger** — Endgame starts when any player has claimed 6 point cards.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Trigger** — Endgame starts when any player has claimed 5 point cards.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Trigger** — Endgame starts when any player has claimed 5 point cards.

#### Round Completion
- **Fair turns** — Finish the round so all players take the same number of turns.

#### Final Scoring Sources
- **Point cards** — Score all printed VP on claimed point cards.
- **Gold coins** — Score 3 VP per Gold Coin.
- **Silver coins** — Score 1 VP per Silver Coin.
- **Leftover cubes** — Convert remaining spices using the game’s leftover-spice conversion rule.

#### Winner & Tiebreak
- **Primary winner** — Highest total VP wins.
- **Tiebreak 1** — If tied, more remaining spice cubes wins.
- **Final tie** — If still tied, tied players share victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### First 3 Turns Priorities
- **Engine first** — Buy at least one card that upgrades or efficiently converts yellow into red/green.
- **Tax discipline** — Avoid paying 2+ skip cubes early unless the target card is a true core engine card.
- **Early claim pace** — Aim to claim a medium point card before chasing a perfect high-value combo.

#### Rest Timing
- **Planned rest** — Rest when your played pile contains multiple high-value cards worth replaying together.
- **Avoid panic rest** — Do not rest just because one hand is awkward if a legal productive play still exists.

#### Resource Quality
- **Upgrade early** — Convert yellow cubes upward before your 10-cube cap forces discard.
- **Target colors** — Prioritize builds that reliably produce green/brown for high VP cards.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Yellow hoarding** — Keeping too many low-value cubes causes cap pressure and wasted actions.
- **Card mismatch buys** — Taking a strong-looking card that does not improve your next 2-3 turns slows your engine.
- **Over-resting** — Frequent rests reduce total productive turns across the game.
- **Ignoring coin race** — Delaying early claims often forfeits easy coin VP to opponents.
- **Tunnel vision on big cards** — Chasing only top-value point cards can create dead turns and lose tempo races.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Engine Growth vs Immediate Scoring
- **Engine growth line** — Invest in better future turns at the cost of short-term VP pace.
- **Immediate scoring line** — Take earlier claims and coins at the cost of long-term ceiling.

#### Decision 2: Deep Merchant Buy vs Cube Conservation
- **Deep buy line** — Pay skip cubes to secure premium card text now.
- **Conserve line** — Keep cubes for near-term claims and avoid feeding opponents row cubes.

#### Decision 3: Rest Now vs One More Action
- **Rest now** — Choose this when your next cycle creates a high-value chain immediately.
- **Delay rest** — Choose this when one more legal play advances cube quality or claim timing.

metadata.notes: Primary PDF text extraction failed in provided source package; content compiled from official publisher rules references and standard Century: Spice Road rules framework.
